title: Bounded Operations Visibility Schema Deployment Preflight Execution
objective: Inspect the live database state and the `09` Operations Visibility schema tranche against current Supabase change and security constraints, then produce a bounded execution readiness verdict before any schema mutation is attempted.
dependency_note: Packet 043 selected the `09` Operations Visibility schema tranche as the first truthful post-041 slice. Packet 044 exists to verify live database state and execution readiness before any schema mutation, especially because this Supabase project exposes the `public` schema through the Data API and the `09` file defines multiple public views.
preflight_checks: Verify whether the target `09` columns, views, and enum additions are already present in the live database.
Inspect the migration file for public-view security posture appropriate to the current Supabase/Postgres environment.
Attempt the normal Supabase advisor path and record whether that tooling is available from this session.
Return a bounded readiness verdict instead of mutating schema if any material blocker is found.
constraints: Do not apply migrations or execute DDL in this packet.
Do not mutate package manifests, lockfiles, runtime services, or old-clone state.
Do not widen the current Olares operating boundary or AI surface.
Do not claim execution readiness if advisor/security review cannot be completed honestly.

execution_evidence.live_db_connection_path: Connected through the session pooler using the stored Supabase credentials and the database MCP server.

execution_rationale: Live database inspection returned zero matching `09` tranche columns, zero matching operations views, and zero matching enum additions, so the tranche is not already deployed and would land as new schema rather than as a no-op reconciliation.
`PROJECT_STATUS.md` also marks this tranche as deploy-next rather than already live, which matches the database inspection result.

`Supabase/schema/09_schema_additions.sql` defines multiple public views with plain `CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW` statements and no `WITH (security_invoker = true)` treatment, while this Supabase project exposes the `public` schema and PostgreSQL 17 supports `security_invoker` for views.

The normal advisor path could not be completed honestly from this session: Supabase MCP advisor calls failed with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'invoke')`, and the local `supabase` CLI is not installed.
blocked_by: The `09_schema_additions.sql` public views are not yet authored with an explicit `security_invoker` posture suitable for an exposed Supabase `public` schema.
The normal Supabase advisor path is unavailable from this session, so execution readiness cannot be claimed with the intended advisor/security review complete.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 045 - Operations Visibility Schema Tranche Security Invoker And Advisor-Path Remediation
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the selected first post-041 Operations Visibility preflight slice. Packet 044 connected to the live Supabase database through the session pooler, verified that the `09` tranche is not yet deployed, and found that `09_schema_additions.sql` defines public views without an explicit `security_invoker` posture while the project exposes the `public` schema. Because the normal advisor path was also unavailable in this session, Packet 044 closes as not execution-ready and routes to a narrow remediation packet rather than authorizing schema mutation.
next_action: Execute `Olares Dev Residency 045 - Operations Visibility Schema Tranche Security Invoker And Advisor-Path Remediation` before any attempt to apply the `09` schema tranche.
